response.justification: The candidate brings genuinely relevant geospatial experience (founder of an open-source geo-data platform, GIS work for Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems work handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and caching. However, that systems work was in DLang, not the required modern C++ or Rust — his C++ experience is brief and dates to 2015 — and he has no routing or navigation-specific background. Recent roles are TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, which is a step away from this pipeline-focused systems role. Worth a conversation only if the team is open to a strong systems generalist ramping up on C++.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain experience: founded and operates an open-source geo-data mapping platform used in production
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Broad stack adaptability and strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, Linux, Docker, Kubernetes)
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience (a ~7-month role in 2014-2015) and no Rust; primary systems language is DLang
No routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience — geospatial work was mapping/data collection, not routing
Recent years focused on TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, distant from large-scale data pipeline engineering
